items.title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence and more
items.summary: Apple’s WWDC 2026 event kicked off this morning at 10 a.m. PT at Apple Park, starting a week full of expected announcements around Siri, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence and more, along with developer events and demos. This year’s event is particularly notable for a couple things. It marks CEO Tim Cook’s last with the company, […]

items.title: Apple just taught your iPhone to finish your sentences, your photos, and your workflows
items.summary: Apple is adding new AI-powered features to Safari, Shortcuts, and Password apps.

items.title: Apple will let you build workflows using AI in its new Shortcuts app
items.summary: Shortcuts gets an AI upgrade, letting you describe the workflow you want in a prompt.

items.title: WhatsApp says it disrupted new NSO spyware phishing attacks
items.summary: WhatsApp has detected and stopped spear-phishing campaigns allegedly conducted by the NSO Group after investigating user reports of social engineering attacks. [...]

items.title: Apple’s Image Playground doesn’t suck anymore
items.summary: Apple's AI image generator is getting a makeover that could make it more competitive.

items.title: Apple’s Photos app is getting new AI editing features
items.summary: A new spatial "Reframe" feature will let users use AI to adjust perspectives.

items.title: Apple gives Siri its own dedicated app
items.summary: Siri is finally getting its own app.

items.title: Apple is fixing the headache of splitting the bill with its new Siri in Camera feature
items.summary: "If you're grabbing a bite with friends and point your iPhone at the bill, then [you can] select what you ordered to split the tab with Apple Cash," said Apple VP of Software Sebastien Marineau-Mes.

items.title: Apple’s long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here
items.summary: The idea behind the new "Siri AI" is to turn the assistant from a voice controlled assistant into an AI companion that can do a lot more.

items.title: Amazon is launching AI-generated custom merch
items.summary: Amazon is expanding its print-on-demand features to AI-generated designs created using Alexa for Shopping for products like T-shirts, water bottles, and hoodies. Shoppers can use text prompts to generate images that are then printed on to blanks for sale on Amazon. They can then share the link to the design so other people can buy […]

items.title: Apple announces Siri AI and its next generation of Apple Intelligence
items.summary: Two years after first revealing its plans for Apple Intelligence and a smarter Siri that never fully materialized, at WWDC, Apple just revealed a new set of AI features and a smarter, more personalized Siri. Apple calls Siri AI an "entirely new version of Siri" and says it's both more conversational and more capable than […]

items.title: Armenia’s pro-Europe party wins election despite Russia-linked disinformation
items.summary: Pashinyan's Civil Contract party won nearly 50% of Sunday's vote, defeating the pro-Russian Strong Armenia party led by Russian-Armenian billionaire Samvel Karapetyan, which received around 23% of the vote.

items.title: Meta Blocks NSO Group's New WhatsApp Phishing Attack, Files Contempt Order
items.summary:
Meta on Monday said it detected and blocked spear-phishing attempts linked to Israeli spyware vendor NSO Group.

In addition, the tech giant said it's filing a federal court contempt order against the company for violating a permanent injunction that barred it from targeting WhatsApp and its users.

"They tried to trick people into clicking on malicious links to drive them to external websites

items.title: WhatsApp says NSO targeted users with spearfishing attacks in violation of court order
items.summary: WhatsApp said it is filing a federal court contempt order against NSO for violating a permanent injunction that bars it from mounting attacks against its users.

items.title: Gogs patches critical zero-day enabling remote code execution
items.summary: Gogs has patched a critical security zero-day flaw that can allow attackers to compromise Internet-facing instances and access any repositories (including private ones). [...]

items.title: 'Hades' Campaign Against PyPI Puts New Spin on Shai-Hulud
items.summary: The latest attacks, which hit 37 PyPI wheels and 19 code packages, show a continued evolution of the persistent software supply chain threat.

items.title: NotebookLM’s Gemini 3.5 upgrade adds a cloud computer and help finding sources
items.summary: Google is rolling out "across the board" updates to NotebookLM. Launched in 2023, NotebookLM allows you to interact with your notes and sources using AI, […]

items.summary: Attackers can chain three already fixed vulnerabilities in the Ubiquiti UniFi OS server to execute remote code with root privileges and without authentication. [...]

items.summary: A new feature in the Amazon Shopping app allows users to generate designs with Alexa, then print them on products like T-shirts, hoodies, and tumblers.

items.title: Critical Check Point VPN Flaw Exploited to Bypass Passwords in IKEv1 Setups
items.summary:
Check Point has warned of active exploitation of a critical vulnerability impacting Remote Access VPN and Mobile Access deployments that are configured to use the deprecated IKEv1 key exchange protocol.

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50751 (CVSS score: 9.3), is a case of a logic flow weakness in certificate validation that allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to bypass user

items.title: Russia upgrades rules for its digital spy system to better track citizens online
items.summary: New regulations published by Russia's Ministry of Digital Development at the end of May updated the technical standards governing SORM, formally known as the System for Operative Investigative Activities.

items.title: Reducing security operations complexity with Wazuh Cloud

items.title: AI Phishing Is Crushing SOCs with Alert Volume: How to Reduce Tier 1 Overload
items.summary:
Phishing has always been a numbers game. AI has turned it into a volume machine.

Attackers can now create convincing emails, fake login pages, and tailored lures in minutes. Every polished message adds another case for Tier 1 to review, another link to inspect, and another alert that cannot be dismissed at a glance.

As the queue grows, a credential theft attempt or malware delivery can easily

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Instagram Account Hacks, Android Zero-Day, GitHub Worm and More
items.summary:
Monday again. The weekend was meant to be quiet. It wasn't. Last week had poisoned packages, a broken AI helper, and a worm tearing through repos. The ugly part: basic tricks still worked.

A chatbot got fooled. A bot token got leaked inside the malware. The same old mistakes showed up again. And while everyone chased the loud stuff, quieter attackers sat in inboxes for months, reading mail and

items.title: The Hardest Fork
items.summary: Mythos is real. I know a big chunk of the industry thinks it's a marketing stunt, and I get why. I get it. But I've seen the findings, and they're bad. These aren't "whoops, this line right here is wrong, and that's RCE." They're novel combinations of a few dozen issues out of thousands of things every SAST scanner already finds, chained together into something much worse. It's real creativity,

items.title: VerdantBamboo Deploys BSD Variant of BRICKSTORM on Linux Appliances
items.summary:
A China-nexus cyber espionage group has been observed deploying a BSD variant of a known backdoor called BRICKSTORM, as well as two other malware families codenamed PLENET (aka GRIMBOLT) and AGENTPSD to target Linux systems.

The activity has been attributed by Volexity to a threat cluster it tracks as VerdantBamboo, which it said overlaps with hacking groups known as Clay Typhoon (Microsoft),

items.title: UNC3753 Used Vishing and Physical Intrusions in U.S. Data Theft Extortion Campaign
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a financially motivated data theft extortion campaign that has targeted dozens of organizations across professional, legal, and financial services in the U.S. between January and May 2026.

The activity has been attributed by Google Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) to a threat actor dubbed UNC3753, which is also known as

items.title: VS Code Adds 2-Hour Extension Auto-Update Delay to Limit Supply Chain Attacks
items.summary:
Microsoft has announced that Visual Studio Code (VS Code) will apply a two-hour delay before extensions for the integrated development environment (IDE) are updated automatically to a newer version in an attempt to tackle software supply chain threats.
